concepts.title: Sign Convention
concepts.conceptBody:
Sign Convention

We have found students often confused over the sign of ‘$\text{g}$’. 

As per our sign convention (positive upwards and negative downwards) it is always negative, whether the particle is moving upwards or downwards. 

Now if $\text{u}$ is upwards (i.e., $\text{u}\uparrow \downarrow \text{g}$ ) motion is retarded and if $\text{u}$ is either zero or downwards (u $\downarrow \downarrow $ g) motion is accelerated.

Assertion: The position-time graph of a uniform motion in one dimension of a body can have negative slope.
Reason : If the body is moving in the opposite direction of positive motion, then its speed is negative.
